input.headline: Manchester derby live on Sky in January as fixtures released up to February 2

raw.headline: Manchester derby live on Sky in January as fixtures released up to February 2

winner: I heard Sky is broadcasting the Manchester derby with such clarity now, you can almost hear the players' heartbeats—unless they're running too fast for the signal to catch up.

candidates.text: Why did the Manchester derby player bring a ladder to the match? Because he heard the fixtures were *raised* to new heights!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative wordplay on 'raised' with a specific reference to the derby and fixtures, concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: I tried to watch the Manchester derby on Sky, but my remote control kept saying "Come back in January"—apparently even the satellites are tired of waiting for the fixtures to be released.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with satellites being tired, specific reference to Sky and fixtures delay, concise and natural humor."}

candidates.text: I'll be watching the Manchester derby on Sky in January, though I'm still waiting for my remote control to come out of its February 2nd hibernation.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative use of 'February 2nd hibernation' adds a humorous twist, ties well with the headline, and is concise and specific."}

candidates.text: I heard Sky is broadcasting the Manchester derby with such clarity now, you can almost hear the players' heartbeats—unless they're running too fast for the signal to catch up.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific comparison to signal quality, with a clever twist on the clarity of broadcast."}

candidates.text: The fans are so excited for the January fixtures that they’ve already started mourning the fact that the February ones are already over.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on fan excitement and timing, concise and specific to the context."}

candidates.text: The Manchester derby is scheduled for January, so I guess you could say the fixtures are finally getting some "sky-high" attention.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with the pun on 'sky-high' referencing both Sky (the broadcaster) and the excitement of the derby. It's concise and specific, though the humor is somewhat mild and relies on a familiar pun."}

candidates.text: The Manchester derby will be broadcast live on Sky in January — just in time for everyone to forget about it by February 2nd.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses specific details from the headline, and delivers a mild surprise with its twist on timing. However, it lacks deeper originality and relies slightly on a predictable punchline."}

candidates.text: Sky Sports announced they'd be showing the Manchester derby in January—then their CEO said he'd be watching it from February.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke has a mild twist with the CEO's comment, but it lacks specificity and surprise. It also feels slightly forced in its setup."}

candidates.text: The Manchester derby will be broadcast live on Sky in January, but unfortunately, the stadium is still being held for the fixtures released up to February 2.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke has some wordplay with 'held for the fixtures,' but it's slightly confusing and lacks clear punchline surprise."}
